Do not edit this file directly. -->  # VER-QUIC-CRT-0009 - QUIC Client Certificate Acceptance Policy Verification  ## Scope  This slice verifies the permanent client-side certificate-acceptance policy seam and the tightened peer transport-parameter commit gate. It does not prove certificate trust, hostname validation, certificate-path validation, revocation, 0-RTT, 1-RTT, key update, or server-role handshake crypto.  ## Requirements Verified  - REQ-QUIC-CRT-0103 - REQ-QUIC-CRT-0109 - REQ-QUIC-CRT-0111  ## Verification Method  Focused requirement-home tests and inspection evidence for the client-side certificate-acceptance seam, its pinned-fingerprint policy, and the tightened peer transport-parameter commit gate.  ## Preconditions  - The Incursa.Quic solution builds successfully. - The tests project can access internal library seams through the existing friend assembly relationship.  ## Procedure or Approach  - Run the CRT requirement-home tests that cover the default-deny path, pinned-fingerprint positive and negative paths, explicit acceptance-state behavior, runtime propagation, and the tightened commit gate. - Run the RFC9001 requirement-home tests that still cover inbound Handshake packet processing, outbound Handshake packet emission, malformed-frame rejection, tampered-packet rejection, and wrong-material rejection for the bridge seam. - Inspect the bridge state and runtime integration to confirm that PeerCertificatePolicyAccepted remains explicit, no-policy configurations stay blocked, and PeerTransportParametersCommitted cannot occur before proof, policy acceptance, and peer Finished verification all exist.  ## Expected Result  The bridge accepts a pinned leaf SHA-256 fingerprint only after the peer CertificateVerify proof exists, leaves no-policy configurations blocked without silent acceptance, rejects mismatched pinned fingerprints through the existing fatal path, surfaces PeerCertificatePolicyAccepted explicitly when the pin matches, and does not allow peer transport-parameter commit until proof, policy acceptance, and peer Finished verification all exist. PeerHandshakeTranscriptCompleted remains a transcript milestone, not a trust or authentication claim.  ## Evidence  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/CRT/REQ-QUIC-CRT-0103.cs - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/CRT/REQ-QUIC-CRT-0109.cs - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/CRT/REQ-QUIC-CRT-0111.cs - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/CRT/REQ-QUIC-CRT-0022.cs - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/INT/REQ-QUIC-INT-0003.cs - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9001/REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S8-0002.cs - src/Incursa.Quic/QuicTlsTransport.cs - src/Incursa.Quic/QuicTransportTlsBridgeState.cs - src/Incursa.Quic/QuicTlsTransportBridgeDriver.cs - src/Incursa.Quic/QuicTlsTranscriptProgress.cs - src/Incursa.Quic/QuicConnectionRuntime.cs - src/Incursa.Quic/QuicHandshakeFlowCoordinator.cs - src/Incursa.Quic/QuicTlsKeySchedule.cs - src/Incursa.Quic/QuicTlsCipherSuiteProfile.cs  ## Status  planned  ## Related Artifacts  - SPEC-QUIC-CRT - ARC-QUIC-CRT-0009 - WI-QUIC-CRT-0009
